newsSinceJun6: No new company-specific fundamentals since June 6. Today's -8.9% move is flow-driven: profit-taking after recent record highs, technical sell signals, and broad weakness across optical communications names, with sentiment still soured by the already-known fiscal Q3 Datacom EML supply-constraint narrative (second-source Lumentum lasers qualified; supply relief expected more in the September quarter). 1.6T/Blackwell positioning and the 2M sq ft capacity build (on schedule for year-end 2026) are unchanged.

sources.label: TipRanks - Why Fabrinet's Hot Stock Is Suddenly Sinking
Seeking Alpha - Fabrinet falls after Q3 results, analysts mull Datacom supply constraints
Investing.com - Fabrinet at 2026 Optical Fiber Conference transcript

verdicts.rankRationale: An -8.9% flow-driven flush with the 1.6T/Blackwell manufacturing thesis intact improves asymmetry on an alpha list that penalizes post-rerating chase; YTD is now a modest +18.6%, so the prior crowding discount is partly worked off. FN stays peripheral to the core passive/power bottleneck stack, so the move up is small.
verdicts.bull: Sole-source-like share in 1.6T transceivers for NVIDIA Blackwell plus a 2M sq ft capacity build gives multi-year volume leverage; a 9% one-day discount on no new news is exactly the entry an alpha list should reward.
verdicts.neutral: FN is AI-optics adjacent rather than a passive/power bottleneck, so even improved asymmetry only justifies a modest re-rank on this page.
verdicts.bear: Datacom EML supply constraints linger into the September quarter and customer concentration in NVIDIA means any hyperscaler optics digestion hits hard; the technical breakdown could extend.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: EML supply relief (Lumentum second source) flowing through the September quarter and 1.6T ramp volumes.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Datacom supply constraints persisting or NVIDIA optics demand digestion turning the flow-driven sell-off into a fundamental one.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's move down was driven by crowding and the rerated YTD; today's -8.9% washout on no new fundamentals partially de-crowds the name and restores some latent asymmetry, warranting a small reversal at the margin. It remains a widely-owned, well-understood optical AI beneficiary, so it stays deep in the list.
verdicts.bull: If the flush shakes out momentum holders, FN's 100% 1.6T share for Blackwell and on-schedule capacity expansion make it cheaper hidden-leverage on the 1.6T transition than the consensus label implies.
verdicts.neutral: One down day does not make a consensus optical name under-owned; the connectivity & photonics thesis and Medium conviction are unchanged.
verdicts.bear: Crowded ownership cuts both ways: continued optics-sector de-risking could compound the technical damage regardless of fundamentals.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: 800G/1.6T manufacturing volumes and visible supply improvement in the September quarter.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind across optical AI names extending the drawdown; margin pressure if constrained Datacom mix persists.

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 15: the -8.9% drop is flow-driven and the scale-out optics thesis (1.6T, CPO-adjacent, Blackwell transceivers) is intact, which slightly improves the score, but no new fundamental evidence justifies leapfrogging names with more direct bottleneck criticality.
verdicts.bull: Record manufacturing position in 1.6T transceivers, robust and increasing demand per management at OFC 2026, and the year-end 2026 capacity completion underpin the scale-out bandwidth thesis at a now-cheaper price.
verdicts.neutral: YTD is down to +18.6% after today, easing the prior rerating concern flagged in the June 8 audit; setup is better but the evidence set is unchanged.
verdicts.bear: Operating cash flow softness, higher capex, and Datacom supply constraints flagged around fiscal Q3 remain the live execution risks the page thesis already watches.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Fiscal Q4 (June quarter) results and September-quarter EML supply relief confirming the 1.6T ramp.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Execution/margin slippage in the 1.6T ramp or a sustained optical-sector demand reset.
